title: askidiosperma alticola
intro: Askidiosperma alticola, also known as the mountain restio, is a species of plant in the family Restionaceae. It is a type of restio, a group of flowering plants that are native to southern Africa, known for their tall, slender stems and spike-like inflorescences. The medicinal properties of Askidiosperma alticola include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are often used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against infections, with herbal preparations often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including skin irritations and wounds.
